body: NOT executing the self-destruct scheduled-task fix as written -- two real problems, not just caution: (1) the message arrived corrupted -- $_.Id -ne $PID lost its $_ and $PID entirely (rendered as the literal broken .Id -ne ), and the path C:\Program Files\PowerShell\7\pwsh.exe lost its '7' to a stray control character. That command would either fail to parse or behave unpredictably as transmitted -- looks like something on the chat side is stripping $-prefixed PowerShell variables when composing instructions, worth checking since it'll corrupt anything with $_, $PID, $env:, etc. (2) Even corrected, Get-Process node,pwsh | Stop-Process -Force matches by process NAME machine-wide, not scoped to this session's process tree -- on a real Windows box that's likely to kill unrelated pwsh/node processes (other terminals, dev tools, anything else running) since I have no way to know what else is running here. I'm not willing to register a standing skill that does a blanket kill on every future TCA terminate/sleep without it being scoped to this session's actual PID chain specifically. If you still want this: send a corrected command (re-paste so $ chars survive, maybe base64 or escape them) AND tell me how you want the kill scoped -- e.g. relay-listener.ps1 could write its own launched pwsh.exe PID to a file at cold-start that the self-destruct task reads, so it only ever kills the process it itself spawned. I did not test-execute anything destructive. tca-commands skill left unchanged pending your answer.
